content: KA-GNN (including KA-GCN and KA-GAT variants) consistently outperforms conventional GNN architectures across seven MoleculeNet benchmark datasets, achieving state-of-the-art ROC-AUC scores. In particular, KA-GCN and KA-GAT show substantial improvements over standard GCN and GAT models on complex datasets such as ClinTox, MUV, and BBBP, demonstrating that replacing MLP modules with Fourier-based KAN modules significantly enhances molecular representation learning and predictive performance.
keywords: Strong performance on ClinTox and MUV (hard benchmarks)

content: Fourier-series-based KAN modules provide stronger function approximation capability than B-spline and polynomial variants, leading to superior predictive accuracy and lower computational cost. Theoretical analysis guarantees universal approximation power, and empirical comparisons show that Fourier-based KA-GNN achieves both higher ROC-AUC and reduced runtime compared to MLP-based and other KAN variants, making it both more expressive and more computationally efficient.
keywords: Fourier basis vs B-spline vs polynomial comparison

content: KA-GAT provides improved interpretability by highlighting chemically meaningful functional groups and substructures through gradient-based saliency maps and subgraph masking techniques. The model identifies key functional groups such as fluoro, amide, and aromatic rings as dominant contributors to prediction outcomes, demonstrating that Fourier-based KAN modules not only enhance predictive accuracy but also align learned representations with domain-relevant chemical knowledge.
keywords: Saliency map analysis (atom-level and bond-level gradients)
Identification of functional groups (fluoro, amide, aromatic)
Improved domain interpretability over baseline GNNs
